title: Ctenitis lanuginosa
intro: Ctenitis lanuginosa, also known as the woolly ctenitis fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, antimicrobial properties, and antioxidant activity, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis lanuginosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitis lanuginosa is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Ctenitis lanuginosa is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This species falls under the genus Ctenitis, which is a genus of ferns that are native to various parts of the world. The classification of Ctenitis lanuginosa is further broken down into its species classification, which includes the genus, species, and sometimes subspecies. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Ctenitis lanuginosa is an evergreen fern that is found in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Ctenitis lanuginosa is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. Its leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties.
